evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor.getTotalFrequency declares 'throws IOException' but internally catches all Exception and returns 0L, with Javadoc explicitly warning: 'a 0 return from a failed lookup will silently corrupt logDice scores for the calling context' — the declared contract (throws) diverges from the actual contract (swallows).

ExploreOptions record has an 'executor' field typed as QueryExecutor, suggesting callers can inject an executor. But exploreByPattern never reads opts.executor() — it always uses this.executor. All call sites pass null. The field is a silent dead parameter in the contract.

WordSketchApiServer.handleSemanticField calls Double.parseDouble and Integer.parseInt on query params without NumberFormatException handling, unlike handleSemanticFieldExplore which wraps the equivalent block in try/catch with a 400 response — inconsistent error contract across sibling handler methods.

BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateLemma is the new clean API delegating to the deprecated extractCollocateFromSnippet — so the non-deprecated method is a thin wrapper around a deprecated implementation, blurring the deprecation contract.

GrammarConfigLoader.throwMissing is declared as returning String (to satisfy constructor-chaining type rules) but always throws IOException — the method signature lies about its return type by necessity; callers cannot observe this but it creates an unusual contract shape.

QueryExecutor interface documents findCollocations as ranking 'by logDice', but getTotalFrequency returning 0L on failure means logDice can be computed as 0.0 for all collocates when the headword lookup fails — the 'ranked by logDice' contract is silently degraded without any error propagation to callers.

BlackLabQueryExecutor.findDependencyCollocations is a public method not declared in the QueryExecutor interface — it is not part of the interface contract but is used directly in Main.java via a cast to the concrete type, leaking an implementation method into a consumer.

strengths: QueryExecutor interface has thorough Javadoc distinguishing findCollocations, executeBcqlQuery, executeQuery, and executeSurfacePattern — each method's role is clearly differentiated.
ConcordanceResult.forSnippet factory is honest about what it provides (null fields) and documents the null contracts on the class.
GrammarConfigLoader validates all invariants eagerly at construction time and throws IOException with descriptive messages for every invalid state.
SemanticFieldExplorer.close() correctly implements AutoCloseable with ownsExecutor guard, honouring the resource-management contract.
contract_coherence.issue_character: The central pattern of contract violation is swallowing exceptions and returning sentinel values (0L, null) where the declared contract (throws IOException, non-null Optional) implies errors will surface — callers cannot distinguish 'not found' from 'lookup failed', silently corrupting downstream computations.
contract_coherence.score_rationale: The codebase scores below the midpoint due to the getTotalFrequency throw-vs-swallow mismatch that is self-documented as corrupting logDice, and the ExploreOptions dead executor parameter that makes the record's contract misleading. The QueryExecutor interface contracts are strong, but the implementation undercuts them at the execution layer. This is not a low-scoring codebase — the interface docs and factory methods are good — but the implementation-level contract breaks are real and consequential.

issues.summary: getTotalFrequency declares throws IOException but silently returns 0L on all failures, corrupting logDice scores
evidence: Method signature: 'public long getTotalFrequency(String lemma) throws IOException'.
Implementation: catch (Exception e) { logger.warn(...); return 0L; } — all exceptions are swallowed.
Javadoc explicitly states: 'Note: a 0 return from a failed lookup will silently corrupt logDice scores for the calling context.' — the violation is known and documented but not fixed.
issues.suggestion: Either propagate the exception (remove the catch, let IOException bubble) so callers can handle lookup failures, or change the declared return type contract to Optional<Long> / return -1 as a sentinel that callers explicitly handle. If silent-0 is intentional for resilience, remove 'throws IOException' from the signature to match the true contract.

issues.summary: ExploreOptions.executor is typed as QueryExecutor but is always null and never read by exploreByPattern
evidence: ExploreOptions record declaration: 'public record ExploreOptions(int topCollocates, int nounsPerCollocate, double minLogDice, int minShared, boolean includeExamples, int headPos, int collocatePos, QueryExecutor executor)'.
All call sites pass null as the executor: 'new ExploreOptions(topCollocates, nounsPerCollocate, minLogDice, minShared, false, headPos, collocatePos, null)'.
exploreByPattern never references opts.executor() — it uses this.executor throughout.
issues.suggestion: Remove the executor field from ExploreOptions. If per-call executor injection is ever needed, add it as a method parameter to exploreByPattern directly rather than as a record field.

issues.summary: handleSemanticField parses numeric query params without NumberFormatException handling, unlike sibling handlers
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore wraps equivalent parsing in: 'try { topCollocates = Integer.parseInt(...); ... } catch (NumberFormatException e) { HttpApiUtils.sendError(exchange, 400, ...); return; }' — the contract for callers differs between identical operations on sibling endpoints.
issues.suggestion: Wrap the Double.parseDouble / Integer.parseInt calls in handleSemanticField in the same try/catch pattern used by handleSemanticFieldExplore, returning 400 on parse failure.

issues.summary: BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateLemma delegates to deprecated extractCollocateFromSnippet, blurring the deprecation boundary
evidence: 'static String extractCollocateLemma(String matchXml) { return extractCollocateFromSnippet(matchXml); }' — the 'clean API' method is a thin wrapper that forwards to the method it is meant to replace.

extractCollocateFromSnippet is marked @Deprecated with 'Use extractHeadLemma or extractCollocateLemma for clarity' — but extractCollocateLemma itself calls the deprecated method, making both names aliases for the same behavior rather than a meaningful replacement.
issues.suggestion: Inline the implementation of extractCollocateFromSnippet into extractCollocateLemma (iterate LEMMA_ATTR, return last group). Then the deprecated method can delegate to the new one (reversing the delegation direction) or be deleted if no external callers remain.

root_causes: Resilience-first design in BlackLabQueryExecutor swallows exceptions at the frequency-lookup boundary, breaking the interface contract silently.
ExploreOptions was extended with an executor field for a planned feature that was never implemented, leaving a dead parameter in the public record contract.
